seed: signal vs noise; exposure_vs_shelter; corner_invasion; center_pulse; islands against echo; lit_edges; memory_vs_erasure; ladder | abstract variation request 107; target batch count 500
title: Echoing Signals
reasoning: The emotional tone captures the tension between clarity and interference, reflecting how isolated signals compete within a noisy environment. The palette comprises deep navy and subtle dim cyan representing 'signal', contrasted with black to symbolize 'noise'. The static black background enforces the feeling of isolation, while sporadic bright embers in the blue and cyan create accents that punctuate the dark field, symbolizing fleeting moments of clarity amid chaos. The collision of clean blue channels interspersed with dark noise creates a rhythmic pulse throughout the grid, embodying a struggle for presence and memory within conceptual echoes. Pattern-wise, the grid layout represents a dense island of clarity vying for space against the overwhelming sea of noise, allowing the viewer to interpret the tension visually.

layout.name: island_and_sea
layout.description: A small dense island sits inside a larger field of quieter pixels.

composition.name: signal_vs_noise
composition.idea: One readable pulse tries to survive inside static, interference, and false echoes.

referenceExample.title: Signal Inside Noise
referenceExample.pattern: one readable green path repeatedly interrupted by magenta static

generationRules: Pick 1 pattern type for emotion A.
Pick 1 pattern type for emotion B.
Pick 1 shape seed as the central object.
Apply 1 to 6 broken pixels to create imperfection.
Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the mood is panic, rage, urgency, euphoria, or pressure.
Use the shape seed as an abstract organizing metaphor, not as a literal icon.
Make the two selected pattern types visibly collide, overlap, interrupt, or trade space.
Prefer 4 to 8 distinct hex colors, with at least one quiet field color and one accent color.
Keep the 8x8 grid readable at thumbnail size by preserving clear large-scale structure.
Let one emotion dominate 55 to 70 percent of the square unless the selected layout demands balance.
Use broken pixels as intentional disruptions, scars, repairs, leaks, or false echoes.
Avoid perfectly even distribution unless the chosen composition is control, serenity, clarity, or certainty.
Give the main collision a location: center, edge, corner, diagonal, border, or channel.
generationSpec.negativeSpaceRule: Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the chosen emotional tone needs dense pressure.
